issue_types.label: Uses a deprecated model (transitive)
issue_types.description: A non-deprecated model+version transitively depends on a deprecated one. If A uses B and B uses C, and C is deprecated, then both A and B are flagged. The shortest path to the deprecated target is shown.

issue_types.label: Circular dependency
issue_types.description: The model+version is part of a dependency cycle (strongly connected component of size > 1, or a self-loop). Cycles between Catena-X models break code-generation and resolution order.

issue_types.label: Unresolved dependency (not in catalog)
issue_types.description: The model references another `name@version` via an `ext-*:` prefix, but that target is absent from the catalog (the model+version is not present in the upstream repository).

issue_types.label: Missing files (metadata.json / ttl / version)
issue_types.description: Filesystem scan: a version folder is missing a `.ttl` file, a `metadata.json`, or both; or the model folder has no version subfolder at all (`version`).

issue_types.label: Orphan element (isolated, no edge)
issue_types.description: An element has no edge at all — neither incoming nor outgoing. It's defined in the .ttl but not connected to anything in the model graph.

issue_types.label: Orphan element (unreachable from Aspect)
issue_types.description: The element is connected somewhere but can't be reached by following edges starting from the model's `Aspect`. Ignored for `shared.*` models, which have no Aspect by design.

issue_types.label: Depends on an older version (newer release exists)
issue_types.description: The model+version depends on `dep@vX` while a newer **release** `dep@vY` (vY > vX) exists in the catalog. Consider migrating to the latest released version of the dependency.

issue_types.label: Older release while a newer release exists
issue_types.description: This model+version is marked `release` but a newer `release` of the same model exists. Older releases should typically be moved to `deprecated` once a newer one is published.

issue_types.label: Depends on a draft model (unstable contract)
issue_types.description: The model+version depends on another model whose status is `draft`. Draft models have unstable contracts — consumers should wait for a `release` before taking a hard dependency.

issue_types.label: Depends on a model with undefined status
issue_types.description: The model+version depends on another model whose `metadata.json` is absent or malformed (status `undefined`). The lifecycle of the dependency is unknown.

issue_types.label: Aspect with no properties
issue_types.description: An `Aspect` declares an empty `samm:properties` list — it carries no payload at all. Either it's a stub, or the properties list was accidentally emptied.

issue_types.label: Property without characteristic
issue_types.description: A `Property` has no `samm:characteristic`. Properties without a characteristic can't be code-generated (no data type).

issue_types.label: Characteristic without dataType
issue_types.description: A `Characteristic` (not a `Trait`, `Constraint` or other specialized subtype) is missing `samm:dataType`. Pure `Characteristic` elements must declare a datatype to be usable.

issue_types.label: Trait without constraint
issue_types.description: A `samm-c:Trait` defines no `samm-c:constraint`. A Trait without constraints is pointless — it's equivalent to its underlying `baseCharacteristic`.

issue_types.label: Property defined but never used
issue_types.description: A `Property` is declared in the file but never referenced by any `samm:properties` collection (directly or via a bnode wrapper). Likely dead code.

issue_types.label: Property/Event/Operation name not camelCase
issue_types.description: SAMM convention: Property/Event/Operation local names should be `camelCase` (start lowercase). Names violating the convention break tooling that relies on it.

issue_types.label: Aspect/Entity/Characteristic name not PascalCase
issue_types.description: SAMM convention: Aspect/Entity/Characteristic/Constraint local names should be `PascalCase` (start uppercase).

issue_types.label: Element with no description
issue_types.description: The element has no `samm:description` literal at all. Generated documentation (HTML, JSON Schema description, OpenAPI) will be blank for this element.

issue_types.label: Element with no preferredName
issue_types.description: The element has no `samm:preferredName`. Tooling falls back to the raw local name, which is less readable for end users.

issue_types.label: Empty/whitespace description
issue_types.description: The element has a `samm:description` but it's empty or contains only whitespace. Same end-user impact as no description.

issue_types.label: Description has no English (@en) version
issue_types.description: The element has at least one description, but none with a `@en` language tag. Catena-X conventions expect English as the primary language.

issue_types.label: Model URN doesn't match file path
issue_types.description: The `@prefix : <urn:samm:io.catenax.X:V#>` in the .ttl doesn't match the on-disk path `io.catenax.X/V/`. Tooling that resolves by URN or by path will see different identities for the same file.

issue_types.label: File name doesn't match Aspect name
issue_types.description: Convention: the `.ttl` file stem matches the local name of its `Aspect` (e.g. `BatteryPass.ttl` ↔ `:BatteryPass a samm:Aspect`). Files with no Aspect (`shared.*`) are not flagged.

issue_types.label: Version folder isn't a valid semver
issue_types.description: The version folder name doesn't match `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. Sorting and 'newer release exists' checks fall back to string ordering for such versions.

issue_types.label: Uses an older SAMM/BAMM meta-model version
issue_types.description: The model uses an older SAMM/BAMM meta-model version than the newest one observed in the catalog. Consider migrating to the current meta-model (e.g. SAMM 2.0.0 → 2.1.0).

issue_types.label: Model has no RELEASE_NOTES.md
issue_types.description: The model folder has no `RELEASE_NOTES.md`. Consumers can't tell what changed between versions.

issue_types.label: Version not documented in RELEASE_NOTES.md
issue_types.description: The model has a `RELEASE_NOTES.md` but no `## [<version>]` section for this specific version. No release date is recorded.

issue_types.label: No generated HTML documentation (gen/)
issue_types.description: No `gen/<Stem>.html` artifact is present upstream for this model+version. The Generated docs sub-tab will be empty.
